message:
feat(spsc_ring_buffer): Cx-first send + recv on SPSC + SPMC (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

High-leverage slice: the SPSC + SPMC channels are the primary
pipeline backbone between tailers, recorder, and downstream
consumers. Without cx threading, a blocked
sender/receiver can only be unblocked by either (a) the
counter-party clearing capacity / producing a value, or (b)
`.close()`. A stuck pipeline on shutdown has to wait for (b),
and callers with `&Cx` couldn't influence the wait at all.

Additions (all strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `SpscProducer::send_with_cx(cx, value)` — Cx-first parallel
    of `send`. Checkpoint at loop top breaks the caller out of
    a blocked `not_full.notified().await`. Returns `Err(value)`
    on pre-cancel or mid-wait cancel so the caller retains
    ownership of the unsent value.

  * `SpscConsumer::recv_with_cx(cx)` — Cx-first parallel of
    `recv`. Returns `None` on pre-cancel or mid-wait cancel —
    matches the closed-and-drained return shape so existing
    callers that check `None` need no changes; they just see
    "channel ended" rather than hanging.

  * `SpmcProducer::send_with_cx(cx, value)` — same pattern for
    the broadcast producer. Checkpoint fires before checking
    first_full_queue and before the blocking await.

  * `SpmcConsumer::recv_with_cx(cx)` — same pattern for the
    broadcast consumer.

Behavioral invariants preserved:
  - Legacy behavior on uncancelled cx is bit-for-bit identical
    (checkpoint is a no-op on a live cx).
  - SPMC broadcast semantics preserved: every consumer sees
    every sent value (verified by test).
  - FIFO ordering preserved within a single SPSC channel.
  - Capacity enforcement unchanged — a full queue still blocks
    until capacity is available, just now also unblockable by
    cx cancel.

Regression tests (2 new):

  * `spsc_with_cx_preserves_fifo_order` — sends 3 values via
    `send_with_cx`, receives 3 values via `recv_with_cx`,
    asserts FIFO order preserved.

  * `spmc_with_cx_broadcasts_to_all_consumers` — 2-consumer
    SPMC channel, sends 2 values via `send_with_cx`, asserts
    both consumers receive both values in order via their
    respective `recv_with_cx`.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * spsc_ring_buffer:: — 39 passed / 0 failed (tick 83: 37 ->
    tick 84: 39, +2 new parity tests; all 25 legacy spsc/spmc
    tests still green — proves the Cx-first additions are
    purely additive on the uncancelled path)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (32.64s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

All 4 public async methods on SPSC + SPMC producers/consumers
have Cx-first siblings now. The module's Cx-first trail is
COMPLETE.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
